title: Create rules
content:
Use this endpoint to create automated rules. ## Comparing v1.2 and v1.3 ```xtable |Changes{30%}|v1.2{35%}|v1.3{35%}| |---|---|---| |Endpoint path| /v1.2/optimizer/rule/create/| /v1.3/optimizer/rule/create/| |Request parameter data type |`advertiser_id`: number
`dimension_ids`: number[]|`advertiser_id`: string
`dimension_ids`: string[]| |Response parameter data type|`rule_ids`: number[]|`rule_ids`: string[]| ``` ## Request **Endpoint** **Method** POST **Header** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {Required}|string|Request message type
Allowed format: `"application/json"`. | ``` **Parameters** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |advertiser_id {Required}|string| Advertiser ID | |rules|object[]| List of rules| #|apply_objects {required}|object[]| Objects that you want to apply the rules to. Objects can be campaigns, ad groups, or ads.| ##|dimension {required}|string| Category of the object. Enum values: `CAMPAIGN`, `ADGROUP`, `AD`.| ##|dimension_ids| string[]| IDs of the objects.| ##|pre_condition_type {required}|string| Conditions to filter objects that you want to include.
  • `SELECTED`: Only include those that are selected by IDs.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_CAMPAIGN`: Select all active campaigns.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD_GROUP`: Select all active ad groups.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD`: Select all active ads.
  • ` ALL_ACTIVE_AD_GROUP_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all active ad groups under the selected campaign.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all active ads under selected dimensions
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_CAMPAIGN`: Select all inactive campaigns.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_GROUP`: Select all inactive ad groups
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD`: Select all inactive ads.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_GROUP_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all inactive ad groups under the selected campaign.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all inactive ads under the selected campaign or ad group (specified by `dimension` and `dimension_ids`)
| #|conditions {required}|object[]| Conditions| ##|subject_type {required}|string| Subjects that the condition will be based on. Enum values:
  • `COST`
  • `DAILY_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE`
  • `LIFETIME_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE`
  • `IMPRESSION`
  • `CLICK`
  • `CONVERSION`
  • `CONVERSION_SKAN`
  • `CVR`
  • `CVR_SKAN`
  • `CPA`
  • `CPM`
  • `CPC`
  • `CTR`
  • `COST_PER_RESULT`
  • `RESULT`
  • `RESULT_RATE`
  • `BID_STRATEGY`: `values` must be one of these: `COST_CAP`, `BID_CAP`, `MAX_CONVERSION`, `LOWEST_COST`.
  • `OPTIMIZATION_GOAL`: `values`must be one of these: `CONVERT`, `CLICK`, `SHOW`, `REACH`, `INSTALL`, `IN_APP_EVENT`, `LEAD_GENERATION`, `TWO_SECOND_VIDEO_VIEW`, `SIX_SECOND_VIDEO_VIEW`
  • `OBJECTIVE`: `values`must be one of these: `TRAFFIC`, `APP_PROMOTION`, `WEB_CONVERSIONS`, `VIDEO_VIEWS`, `REACH`, `LEAD_GENERATION`
  • `NAME`
  • `DAYS_SINCE_CREATION`: `match_type` should be one of `GT`, `LT`, `BETWEEN`, `MATCH`.
  • `NO_CONDITION`: Be careful when setting a rule without any conditions to avoid any unintended changes.
  • `ROAS_PURCHASE`
  • `ROAS_IMPRESSION_ADS`
  • `ROAS_PURCHASE_SKAN`
  • `ROAS_COMPLETE_PAYMENT`
  • `ROAS_PURCHASES_ONSITE`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CPA`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CONVERSION`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CVR`
| ##|range_type|string| Time range for the condition. Enum values:
  • `TODAY`
  • `YESTERDAY`
  • `PAST_THREE_DAYS`
  • `PAST_FIVE_DAYS`
  • `PAST_SEVEN_DAYS`
  • `LIFETIME`
| ##|match_type|string| Operator to connect a condition with a value. Enum values: `GT` (greater than), `LT` (less than), `BETWEEN`, `MATCH`. Only when `subject_type` is `NAME`, can below `match_type` be used:
  • `CONTAINS`
  • `NOT_CONTAINS`
  • `START_WITH`
  • `END_WITH`
  • `STRING_EQUAL` | ##|values|string[]| Values for the condition. If `match_type` is `BETWEEN`, include two values in this array

    Note: Verification rules for the `values` field vary with the `subject_type` you pass in. See Verification Rules for values for details.| ##|calculation_type|string|The `subject_type` has to be `COST`. Set to `OF_EACH_OBJECT` by default if this field is not provided.

  • `ALL_OBJECTS`
  • `OF_EACH_OBJECT`
    • | #|actions {required}|object[]| Actions to be taken when the condition is met.| ##|subject_type {required}|string| Type of actions to take. Enum: `TURN_ON`, `TURN_OFF`, `MESSAGE`, `DAILY_BUDGET`, `LIFETIME_BUDGET`, `BID`.| ##|action_type|string| Enum values: `INCREASE`, `DECREASE`, `ADJUST_TO`.| ##|value_type|string| Enum values: `EXACT`, `PERCENT`.| ##|value {+conditional}|object|The budget value.
    Required when `subject_type`=`DAILY_BUDGET`, `LIFETIME_BUDGET` or `BID`.| ###|value {+conditional}|float|The budget value you want to increase, decrease or adjust to.
    • If `action_type` =`INCREASE` or `DECREASE`, value range:[0.01, 1000000000000].
    • If `action_type` =`ADJUST_TO`:
      • when `dimension`=`CAMPAIGN`, value range: [50, 1000000000000].
      • when `dimension`=`ADGROUP`, value range: [20, 1000000000000].
    | ###|limit {+conditional}|float|The maximum budget cap (if `action_type` =`INCREASE`) or minimum budget ( if `action_type` =`DECREASE`).

    Required when `action_type` =`INCREASE` or `DECREASE`.
    • When `dimension`=`CAMPAIGN`, value range: [50, 1000000000000].
    • When `dimension`=`ADGROUP`, value range: [20, 1000000000000].

    If `action_type`=`ADJUST_TO`, you don't need to pass in this field.| ##|frequency_info|object| Frequency settings. It can be used when the action `subject_type` is `DAILY_BUDGET`, `LIFETIME_BUDGET`, or `BID`.| ###|type|string| Enum values:
    • `ONLY_ONCE`
    • `ONCE_IN_24_H`
    • `ONCE_IN_48_H`
    • `ONCE_IN_1_W`
    • `CUSTOM`
    | ###|custom_frequency_type|string|Custom frequency type. Valid only when `type` is `CUSTOM`. Enum:
    • `N_MINTUE_Y_TIMES`
    • `N_HOUR_Y_TIMES`
    • `N_DAY_Y_TIMES`
    | ###|time|number|It is the `N` in `custom_frequency_type`| ###|count|number|It is the `Y` in `custom_frequency_type`| #|notification {required}|object| Notification settings| ##|notification_type {required}|string|Notification type you need. Enum values:
    • `TASK_FINISH`: Notification will be sent when the task is finished. This option is only for `MESSAGE` subject type.
    • `ANY_CHANGES`: If the `subject_type` for the action is not `MESSAGE` but you still want to receive emails, use this option.
    • `NOT_NOTIFICATION`: No notifications are needed.
    | ##|email_setting|object|Email notification settings| ###|notification_period|string|Enum values:
    • `EVERY_TIME`
    • `TIME_SCHEDULE`: notifications will be sent together around a specified time.
    | ###|email_exec_time|string[]|Required when `notification_period` is`TIME_SCHEDULE`. Notification email sent time. For example, "12:00".| ###|no_result_notification|boolean|It will be set as false by default if not provided, meaning no notification will be sent if there is no result.| ###|mute_option|string|This field can be used to mute or unmute email notification for the day. Enum values:
    • `MUTE`
    • `UNMUTE`
    | #|rule_exec_info {required}|object| Rule run settings.| ##|exec_time_type {required}|string| When the rule will be run. Enum values:
    • `PER_HALF_HOUR`: the rule will be run every half hour.
    • `CUSTOM`: the rule will be run at a customized time (specified in the `exec_time` field).
    • `HALF_HOUR_IN_SPECIFIC_TIME_PERIOD`: the rule will be run every 30 minutes during specified time range on specifed days during the week.
    • `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`: the rule will be run only once at a customized time (specified in the `exec_time` field).
    | ##|exec_time|string| If `exec_time_type` is set to `CUSTOM` or `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`, use this field to specify the exact time when the rule will be run.
    Format:
    • "HH:MM" when `exec_time_type` is `CUSTOM`. For example: "12:00".
    • "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM" when `exec_time_type` is `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`. For example: "2023-04-20 04:20".
    Note: If `exec_time_type` = `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`, the `exec_time` cannot exceed one year from the current time. | ##|time_period_info|object[]|Valid when `exec_time_type` is `HALF_HOUR_IN_SPECIFIC_TIME_PERIOD`. It has `start_time`, `end_time`, `date_type` and `num` to specify the time period information.| ###|num{+Conditional}|integers[]|Required when `date_type` is `WEEK` or `MONTH`. Each number in the list represents the day in the week or month.
    • When `date_type` is `WEEK`, value range for this field is [1, 7], meaning Monday to Sunday.
    • When `date_type` is `MONTH`, value range for this field is [1, 28], meaning 1st day to the 28th day of the month.
    | ###|start_time|string| Start time of this time period. For example, "12:00".| ###|end_time|string| End time of this time period. For example, "12:00".| ###|date_type|string|Type of date. Enum values: `DAY`, `WEEK`, `MONTH`.| #|tzone|string|User timezone. In create/update rules, it's used with `mute_option` in notification.email_setting. For acceptable timezone code, see [Time Zone](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737586324313089).| #|name {required}|string| Name of the rule.| |lang|string| Language of the error messages. Enum values: `ZH` (Simplified Chinese), `EN` (English)(default), `JA` (Japanese).| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code curl http) curl --location --request POST 'https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/optimizer/rule/create/' \ --header 'Access-Token: {{Access-Token}}' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "advertiser_id": "{{advertiser_id}}", "rules": [ { "apply_objects": [ { "dimension": "CAMPAIGN", "dimension_ids": [], "pre_condition_type": "ALL_INACTIVE_CAMPAIGN" } ], "conditions": [ { "subject_type": "CLICK", "range_type": "LIFETIME", "match_type": "GT", "values": [ "0" ] } ], "actions": [ { "subject_type": "MESSAGE" } ], "notification": { "notification_type": "TASK_FINISH" }, "rule_exec_info": { "exec_time_type": "CUSTOM", "exec_time": "12:00" }, "name": "[API]Automated Rule-example" } ] }' (/code) ```
    ### Verification Rules for `values` Verification rules for the `values` field vary with the `subject_type` you pass in. Please specify the `values` field according to the rules. Otherwise, an error will occur. See the table below for verification rules in detail. ```xtable |subject_type{50%}|Verification rules for values{50%}| |---|---|---| |NAME|Length range: (0,512]| |COST
    CPA
    NOT_REAL_TIME_CPA
    CPM
    CPC
    COST_PER_RESULT|Precision: determined by the currency of your advertiser account(See [Currency-specific precision](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1772544628718594) for details.)
    Value range: [0,100000000]| |DAILY_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE
    LIFETIME_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE
    CVR
    CVR_SKAN
    NOT_REAL_TIME_CVR
    CTR
    RESULT_RATE|Precision: 0.01
    Value range: [0,100]| |IMPRESSION
    CLICK
    CONVERSION
    CONVERSION_SKAN
    NOT_REAL_TIME_CONVERSION
    RESULT|Precision: 1
    Value range: [0,10000000000]| |ROAS_PURCHASE
    ROAS_IMPRESSION_ADS
    ROAS_PURCHASE_SKAN
    ROAS_COMPLETE_PAYMENT
    ROAS_PURCHASES_ONSITE|Precision: 0.01
    Value range: [0,100000]| ``` ## Response ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |code |number|Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |message |string|Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |request_id|string| Unique ID of the request.| |data|object| Returned data.| #|rule_ids |string[]| IDs of the rules that have been created or updated.| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code JSON JSON) { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "2021072906384801024503714429014AE4", "data": { "rule_ids": [ "6984697088064290818" ] } } (/code) ```
